evals.prompt: Sync `docs/api/use-click.md` with the current implementation in `src/composables/interactions/use-click.ts`. Keep the page concise, verify defaults from source, and keep exact contract details in the API page.
evals.expected_output: An updated `docs/api/use-click.md` that matches the source, keeps the composable API structure, includes a Vue example imported from `v-float`, and cross-links to the Interactions guide and related APIs.
assertions: The page retains `## Type`, `## Details`, `## Example`, and `## See Also` sections.
The Type block documents `UseClickContext` and `UseClickOptions` from source.
The Details section states that `closeOnOutsideClick` defaults to `false` and `outsideClickEvent` defaults to `"pointerdown"`.
The example imports from `v-float` and binds `:style="context.floatingStyles.value"`.
The See Also section links to `/guide/interactions`.

evals.prompt: Rewrite `docs/guide/interactions.md` so modal surfaces are taught as a workflow that combines `useClick`, `useEscapeKey`, and `useFocusTrap`, without duplicating full API contracts from the reference pages.
evals.expected_output: An updated workflow-first guide that keeps a clear step progression, teaches the modal interaction stack on one shared context, and links to the canonical API pages for exact contracts.
assertions: The guide remains step-by-step and keeps a clear progression through the interaction layers.
The modal workflow includes `useClick`, `useEscapeKey`, and `useFocusTrap` on the same shared context.
The guide explains why the pattern is used instead of only listing the raw calls.
The guide links to relevant API pages instead of restating full option interfaces.
The guide uses a VitePress container only where it removes friction or calls out a real gotcha.

evals.prompt: Review `docs/api/offset.md` and `docs/guide/middleware.md` against current VFloat conventions. Fix outdated examples, naming, links, and VitePress issues while preserving the repo's existing middleware API family unless a full family cleanup is necessary.
evals.expected_output: Corrected docs pages that fix concrete issues, preserve the current middleware API family for targeted edits, and keep examples and links aligned with current VFloat conventions.
assertions: `docs/api/offset.md` keeps the current middleware API family style unless the entire middleware family is standardized together.
Any stale `context.floatingStyles` binding is corrected to the right reactive form for the page family being edited.
The guide links to relevant API reference pages.
The review fixes concrete issues instead of only describing them.
